name: Spider Curl
resistance_profile: Lying chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging vertically, the load line is hardest as the forearm approaches horizontal — a strict, peak-contraction-biased curl with no body English.
cues: Keep the upper arms hanging straight down and still
Supinate and squeeze at the very top
Do not let the shoulders shrug the weight up
execution_steps: Set an incline bench to about 45 degrees and lie chest-down on it with your feet braced.
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms forward.
Keeping the upper arms vertical and still, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, supinating as you go.
Squeeze hard at the top where tension peaks, then lower under control to a full hang.
good_when: You want strict biceps work with zero momentum
Chest support keeps the lower back out of it
bad_when: You want a deep loaded stretch at the bottom
No suitable incline bench is available
common_errors: Swinging the upper arms instead of isolating the elbow
Cutting the range short at the top and bottom
progressions: Add a peak-contraction hold at the top
regressions: Perform a seated dumbbell curl instead
